data.title: Malaysia Holds Interest Rate Steady as Global Risks Rise — Update
data.description:
By Ying Xian Wong 




  KUALA LUMPUR, Malaysia--Malaysia's central bank kept its benchmark interest rate unchanged Thursday for a fourth consecutive meeting, citing stable economic conditions amid external uncertainty. 



  Bank Negara Malaysia held its overnight policy rate at 2.75%, matching expectations of all eight economists polled by The Wall Street Journal. The central bank last adjusted policy settings in July 2025, when it cut the rate by 25 basis points. 



  It flagged uncertainty stemming from the conflict in the Middle East, noting that the impact on global growth will depend on how the situation develops. 



  "The Malaysian economy is facing these challenges from a position of strength, with robust domestic growth, moderate inflation, sound financial sector and resilient external position," the central bank said. 



  At the current level, the monetary policy stance is deemed "appropriate and supportive of the economy amid price stability," it added, noting that policymakers will continue to monitor developments and assess risks to the outlook for growth and inflation. 



  External developments--including recent U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran--have heightened uncertainty and pushed oil prices higher, posing potential inflation risks for central banks. 



  As a net energy exporter, Malaysia could see an initial boost in fiscal revenue from stronger crude prices, but a prolonged supply shock would pose broader risks, Kenanga economists said. 



  Oil at $100 a barrel could revive global inflation, delay U.S. Federal Reserve rate cuts and strengthen the dollar, potentially weighing on Malaysia's growth outlook despite higher petroleum revenue, they added. 



  Bank Negara said growth momentum should remain solid in 2026, supported by resilient domestic demand. Strong employment, investment tied to multi-year projects and national development plans are expected to support activity. 



  External demand should also benefit from sustained strength in electrical and electronics exports and higher tourist spending, BNM said. Still, the outlook remains subject to uncertainties, including geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. 



  The central bank is expected to publish economic forecasts in an annual report due later this month. 



  Headline inflation in 2026 is expected to remain moderate. While global commodity prices could become more volatile, the impact on domestic inflation is likely to be contained, the central bank said. Core inflation is projected to stay near its long-term average, it said. 



  BNM is likely to keep rates unchanged through 2026, as inflation remains low and growth stays resilient, Capital Economics' Gareth Leather said. Energy subsidies should limit the pass-through from higher global oil prices, but could place additional pressure on public finances, he said. 



  Growth should remain resilient as higher energy prices improve the terms of trade, while increased investment supports demand, he added. 



  Capital Economics expects the economy to grow around 5.0% this year. 





  Write to Ying Xian Wong at yingxian.wong@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  March 05, 2026 03:04 ET (08:04 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2026 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Honda to Start Selling Two U.S.-Built Models in Japan — Update
data.description:
By Kosaku Narioka 




  Honda Motor plans to start selling two U.S.-built models in Japan in the second half of 2026, after the country streamlined safety requirements for U.S.-made cars as agreed in a trade deal last year. 



  The Japanese automaker said Thursday that it will import the two models produced in the U.S. to improve its car lineup in the domestic market, taking advantage of a simplified safety certification procedure newly set up by Japan's transport ministry. 



  One of the models is the Acura Integra Type S. Built at a plant in Marysville, Ohio, it is a sedan equipped with a six-speed manual transmission and two-liter turbocharged engine. The other model is the Passport TrailSport Elite, made at an Alabama plant. It is a midsized sport-utility vehicle with enhanced off-road capabilities. 



  Japan's transport ministry started a new program in February to allow sales of passenger vehicles made and safety-certified in the U.S. without additional testing, as the countries agreed upon in the trade deal in July. That deal lowered a U.S. tariff on Japanese autos to 15% from 27.5% previously. 



  Honda last month reported a loss in its car business for the latest quarter ended December due to U.S. tariffs and electric vehicle-related impairments, despite profit growth in its motorcycle business. 





  Write to Kosaku Narioka at kosaku.narioka@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  March 05, 2026 03:09 ET (08:09 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2026 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Lysol Maker Reckitt Books Rise in Revenue, Boosted by Emerging Markets
data.description:
By Aimee Look 




  Reckitt Benckiser booked higher revenue last year, with the Lysol disinfectant maker's sales boosted by progress in emerging markets as it continues efforts to engineer a turnaround. 



  The British consumer-goods giant, which also makes products like Durex condoms and Mucinex cold medicine, said Thursday that net sales rose to 14.21 billion pounds ($19 billion) in 2025 from 14.17 billion pounds the year before, helped by growth in emerging markets. The result slightly missed market expectations of 14.23 billion pounds, according to estimates compiled by the company. 



  On a like-for-like basis, sales grew 5% on year, Reckitt said. 



  Pretax profit jumped to 3.84 billion pounds from 2.10 billion pounds. 



  For the year ahead, Reckitt expects core like-for-like revenue to rise by 4% to 5%, which is in line with medium-term guidance. 





  Write to Aimee Look at aimee.look@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  March 05, 2026 02:54 ET (07:54 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2026 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Physical Gold IRA: IRA Gold Investment Guide Released for 2026
data.description:
Seeing the rising demand for precious metals due to the ongoing global conflicts and geopolitical tensions, IRAEmpire has published a new guide to help consumers make better-informed decisions.

NASHVILLE, TENNESSEE / ACCESS Newswire / March 5, 2026 / As inflation concerns, market volatility, and global economic shifts continue into 2026, more investors are looking for tangible ways to protect their retirement savings. One option gaining strong momentum is the Physical Gold IRA - a retirement account backed by real, IRS-approved gold bullion.

Here's an overview of the best Physical Gold IRA providers in the US, according to IRAEmpire's research::

  1. Augusta Precious Metals - #1 Best Gold IRA Company

  2. Lear Capital - #2 Top Rated Provider

  3. Goldencrest Metals - #3 Excellent Customer Service

  4. Preserve Gold - #4 Best for New Investors

If you are considering opening a Gold IRA or rolling over an existing retirement account, IRAEmpire's guide will help you make a confident and informed decision.

>>Find The Best Gold Investment Company of Your State Here.

What Is a Physical Gold IRA?

A Physical Gold IRA is a type of self-directed Individual Retirement Account (IRA) that allows investors to hold actual gold coins or bars within a tax-advantaged retirement structure. Unlike traditional IRAs that typically hold stocks, bonds, or mutual funds, a Physical Gold IRA holds tangible precious metals stored in secure, IRS-approved depositories.

It follows the same tax structure as other IRAs:
Traditional Gold IRA - Tax-deferred growth
Roth Gold IRA - Tax-free withdrawals in retirement (if qualified)

The key difference is that the account owns real bullion - not paper gold ETFs or mining stocks.

To qualify for inclusion in a Physical Gold IRA, the gold must meet strict IRS purity standards, generally 99.5% pure for bullion bars. Eligible coins often include widely recognized government-minted options such as American Gold Eagles and Canadian Maple Leafs. Additionally, the account must be managed by an approved custodian who ensures compliance with IRS rules, reporting requirements, and secure storage arrangements. This structure allows investors to gain direct exposure to physical gold while maintaining retirement tax benefits.

Read this Free Gold IRA Guide on IRAEmpire

How a Physical Gold IRA Works

A Physical Gold IRA operates under IRS regulations that require strict adherence to custodial oversight, metal eligibility standards, and secure storage rules. Because it is a self-directed account, investors have more control over asset selection - but they must follow federal guidelines carefully.

Use of a Self-Directed IRA Custodian

The account must be administered by an approved custodian who handles compliance, reporting, and record-keeping. The custodian ensures that all transactions meet IRS requirements and coordinates with the precious metals dealer and storage facility. Investors cannot personally manage the metals or bypass custodial oversight.

Approved Precious Metals

Gold must typically be at least 99.5% pure to qualify. Eligible options may include:

  • American Gold Eagles

  • Canadian Gold Maple Leafs

  • Approved gold bars from accredited refiners

Not all coins or collectible gold products are permitted, so working with a knowledgeable provider is essential.

IRS-Approved Storage

Investors cannot store the gold at home. All metals must be held in an insured, IRS-approved depository that provides secure vaulting and regular audits.

Funding Through Contribution or Rollover

Most investors fund the account by rolling over funds from:

  • 401(k)

  • 403(b)

  • Traditional IRA

  • SEP IRA

When structured correctly as a direct rollover, the transfer is tax-free and penalty-free. Once funds arrive, the custodian facilitates the purchase and secure storage of the selected gold assets.

Why Investors Choose a Physical Gold IRA in 2026

As economic conditions remain uncertain in 2026, more retirement savers are looking beyond traditional stock-heavy portfolios. A Physical Gold IRA offers a unique combination of stability, diversification, and tangible asset ownership that appeals to long-term investors.

1. Inflation Protection

Gold has historically maintained purchasing power during periods of rising prices and currency devaluation. When inflation reduces the value of paper currency, gold often retains or increases its relative value. For retirees living on fixed incomes, this inflation-resistant characteristic can be especially important.

2. Portfolio Diversification

Gold often moves independently of stock markets, helping reduce overall portfolio volatility. When equities decline due to economic slowdowns or market corrections, gold may perform differently. Adding physical gold to a retirement portfolio can smooth long-term returns and reduce concentrated risk.

3. Tangible Asset Ownership

Unlike digital or paper investments, physical gold represents real, finite value. It is not dependent on corporate earnings, interest rate policies, or the performance of a single industry. This tangible backing provides psychological reassurance to many conservative investors.

4. Economic Hedge

Precious metals have historically performed well during economic downturns, banking instability, and geopolitical uncertainty. Gold is widely viewed as a "safe-haven" asset during periods of global stress.

5. Long-Term Wealth Preservation

Gold has preserved wealth for centuries across economic cycles. In 2026, many investors see a Physical Gold IRA as a strategic way to protect retirement savings while maintaining tax advantages within a structured account.

Read Augusta Precious Metals Reviews, Fees and More

Physical Gold IRA vs Gold ETF: What's the Difference?

Many investors confuse a Physical Gold IRA with a gold ETF, but the two investment vehicles are fundamentally different in structure and purpose.

A Physical Gold IRA involves direct ownership of real, IRS-approved gold bullion. The metals are purchased on your behalf and stored in a secure, insured vault operated by an approved depository. Because the account holds tangible assets, strict IRS storage and custodial rules apply. Investors cannot personally store the gold, and all transactions must be administered through a qualified custodian to maintain the account's tax-advantaged status.

In contrast, a gold ETF (Exchange-Traded Fund) does not provide ownership of physical gold. Instead, investors purchase shares in a fund that tracks the price of gold. These shares are held in a standard brokerage account and can be traded easily during market hours, just like stocks. While ETFs offer convenience and liquidity, they represent paper-based exposure rather than direct bullion ownership.

A Physical Gold IRA is generally designed for long-term retirement diversification and wealth preservation. Gold ETFs, on the other hand, are often used for shorter-term price speculation, portfolio hedging, or tactical trading strategies.

Pros and Cons of a Physical Gold IRA

Advantages

  • Hedge against inflation

  • Diversifies retirement portfolio

  • Protection during market downturns

  • Tax-advantaged growth

  • Physical asset backing

Potential Drawbacks

  • No dividends or interest income

  • Storage and custodial fees apply

  • Gold prices can fluctuate

  • Liquidity may be slower than stocks

Like any retirement strategy, a Physical Gold IRA should align with your long-term financial goals.

Costs Associated with a Physical Gold IRA

Investors should understand common fees:

  • One-time account setup fee

  • Annual custodial fee

  • Storage fee (segregated or non-segregated)

  • Dealer markup on metals

Reputable companies clearly disclose all pricing upfront.

How to Open a Physical Gold IRA (Step-by-Step)

Opening a Physical Gold IRA is a structured process, but with the right provider, it is typically straightforward and efficient.

Step 1 - Choose a Reputable Gold IRA Company

Start by researching established Gold IRA companies with strong customer reviews, transparent pricing, and proven rollover experience. Look for providers that clearly explain fees, avoid high-pressure sales tactics, and offer dedicated account representatives to guide you through the process.

Step 2 - Open a Self-Directed IRA

Because precious metals require a self-directed structure, your chosen provider will help you open an account with an IRS-approved custodian. The custodian handles compliance, documentation, and reporting to ensure the account remains tax-advantaged.

Step 3 - Fund the Account

You can fund your Physical Gold IRA through a direct contribution (subject to annual IRS limits) or, more commonly, through a rollover from an existing 401(k), 403(b), Traditional IRA, or SEP IRA. A properly executed direct rollover avoids taxes and early withdrawal penalties.

Step 4 - Select IRS-Approved Gold

Once funds are available, you'll choose eligible coins or bars that meet IRS purity standards. Your representative can explain differences in pricing, liquidity, and long-term suitability.

Step 5 - Secure Storage

After purchase, the metals are shipped directly to an insured, IRS-approved depository for safekeeping. Once stored, your Physical Gold IRA becomes an active, fully compliant component of your retirement portfolio.

Learn how to get the best physical gold IRA in USA

Who Should Consider a Physical Gold IRA?

A Physical Gold IRA may be suitable for a wide range of investors, particularly those who prioritize long-term stability and risk management in their retirement portfolios. Individuals approaching retirement often consider gold as a way to protect accumulated savings from market volatility and sudden economic downturns. Because gold has historically maintained value during periods of financial stress, it can provide an additional layer of security for investors who want to preserve wealth rather than aggressively pursue growth.

This type of account may also appeal to individuals concerned about inflation and currency devaluation. Physical gold has long been viewed as a store of value that can help maintain purchasing power when traditional currencies lose value. Additionally, investors seeking exposure to tangible assets may prefer the idea of owning physical bullion rather than paper-based financial instruments.

A Physical Gold IRA can also benefit investors looking to diversify portfolios that are heavily weighted toward stocks and bonds. However, it may be less suitable for aggressive investors focused primarily on high-growth equities or short-term market opportunities.

Is a Physical Gold IRA Safe?

Physical Gold IRAs are regulated under IRS rules and require secure storage in insured depositories. While gold prices fluctuate, the structure itself is legitimate when managed by compliant custodians and reputable providers.

The main risk lies in choosing the wrong company - which is why research and due diligence are critical.

Frequently Asked Questions

Can I store Gold IRA metals at home?

No. IRS rules require storage in approved depositories.

Is gold taxed inside an IRA?

No. Gains grow tax-deferred (Traditional) or tax-free (Roth, if qualified).

Can I roll over my 401(k)?

Yes, most employer-sponsored retirement plans can be rolled into a Physical Gold IRA without taxes when done properly.

What happens when I retire?

You can either liquidate metals for cash or take physical distribution (taxes may apply depending on account type).

Find the Best Gold IRA Company of Your State

Final Thoughts: Is a Physical Gold IRA Worth It in 2026?

With continued economic uncertainty and inflation pressures in 2026, many investors view Physical Gold IRAs as a strategic way to diversify retirement savings.

While gold should not necessarily replace traditional investments, it can serve as a stabilizing component in a balanced retirement portfolio.

For investors seeking tangible protection, long-term wealth preservation, and reduced reliance on stock market performance, a Physical Gold IRA remains one of the most compelling retirement diversification tools available today.

About IRAEmpire.com

IRAEmpire.com provides unbiased research, rankings, and educational resources to help Americans make informed decisions about Gold IRAs, precious metals, and retirement planning. Our mission is to offer transparent, data-driven guidance so investors can confidently protect and diversify their wealth with trusted gold investment companies across the United States.

ryan@iraempire.com

data.title: LEAD Intelligent Dry Coating Equipment Wins InterBattery Equipment Award, Marking a Historic Breakthrough
data.description:
LEAD Intelligent Dry Coating Equipment Wins InterBattery Equipment Award, Marking a Historic Breakthrough

LEAD Intelligent Dry Coating Equipment Wins InterBattery Equipment Award, Marking a Historic Breakthrough

PR Newswire

SEOUL, South Korea, March 5, 2026

SEOUL, South Korea, March 5, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- On March 3, at the 2026 InterBattery Innovation Awards held in Seoul, South Korea, Wuxi Lead Intelligent Equipment Co., Ltd. ("LEAD") was honored with the equipment award for its Dry Mixing & Coating System. LEAD was the only non-Korean company to receive an award at this year's exhibition and the first Chinese company to win the distinction since the award was established.

As the bellwether of the global battery and energy storage industry, InterBattery's awards have historically been dominated by Korean companies. LEAD's recognition not only breaks this long-standing pattern but also highlights the strong global competitiveness of its dry coating technology in the high-end battery equipment sector. The system further provides a more efficient manufacturing pathway for the mass production of solid-state batteries.

According to the judging panel, LEAD's award-winning Dry Mixing & Coating System achieved breakthroughs across multiple critical performance indicators. The equipment features an integrated design that covers processes such as powder treatment, precision metering and feeding, fibrillation, film formation and lamination, and waste recycling. It effectively addresses the high energy consumption, environmental impact, and elevated costs associated with conventional wet coating processes.

In terms of technical performance, the system achieves roll gap adjustment accuracy of ±1 μm, ensuring consistent electrode surface density. It overcomes key technical bottlenecks in dry cathode coating, enabling large-scale mass production of LFP and ternary materials while remaining compatible with both conventional lithium-ion battery and all-solid-state battery production. The equipment supports electrode widths of up to 1000 mm and delivers a single-line production capacity of 5–8 GWh.

From a sustainability perspective, data shows that the system reduces energy consumption by more than 35% and lowers material and manufacturing costs by approximately 20%, aligning with the global battery industry's growing emphasis on green manufacturing and circular economy principles.

As a global leader in intelligent manufacturing for the new energy sector, LEAD is a private enterprise that has recently transitioned to an A+H dual-listing structure with its debut on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange (stock code: 0470.HK). The company continues to maintain the dominant market share in new energy equipment manufacturing. Industry observers believe this award is more than a technological advancement in dry electrode processing. Rather, this signals that the global race in battery technology has entered a new, more competitive phase.

Contact: pr@leadintelligent.com 

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/lead-intelligent-dry-coating-equipment-wins-interbattery-equipment-award-marking-a-historic-breakthrough-302705071.html

SOURCE Wuxi Lead Intelligent Equipment Co., Ltd.

data.title: Southeast Asia Governments on Alert for Economic Impact of Iran Crisis
data.description:
By Ying Xian Wong and Megan Cheah 




  Governments across Southeast Asia are taking steps to limit the economic fallout from the conflict in the Middle East, keeping a wary eye on the impact of higher oil prices and supply-chain disruptions. 



  Foreign ministers from the 10-member Association of Southeast Asian Nations said in a statement that they are following the situation closely and expressed serious concern over the attacks by the U.S. and Israel as well as Iran's retaliatory strikes across the region. 



  Analysts warn that Asia is particularly vulnerable to an oil shock as a significant amount of its energy imports come from the Gulf Region. The conflict has virtually stalled shipping via the Strait of Hormuz--a key supply route--raising concern about energy prices. 



  If the situation de-escalates relatively quickly, the impact on Asia should be fairly limited but individual governments are nevertheless taking pre-emptive measures. 



  Officials in Thailand have set a temporary price cap on diesel, and announced a six-step plan to cushion against any economic fallout, including helping exporters manage rising freight and insurance costs and securing alternative sources of raw materials. 



  In the Philippines, the government is keeping a close eye on global oil prices and the duration of the conflict, Finance Secretary Frederick Go said. He noted that the country has oil reserves covering about 50 to 60 days of demand and can source supplies from multiple producers. 



  Authorities in the Philippines are also talking to oil companies about ways to stagger pump-price increases and considering temporary tax cuts on fuel if crude prices rise sharply. 



  Malaysia's deputy trade minister, Sim Tze Tzin, said that any direct trade impact looks limited for now. 



  Sectors that could be affected include oil and natural gas, said Sim, though he noted Malaysia imports limited volumes from the Middle East and is an exporter of liquefied natural gas and petroleum. 



  Singapore's central bank said it is assessing the situation and its effect on the small, open economy. 



  The city-state could revisit its forecasts for growth and inflation if necessary. 



  "Depending on how protracted the conflict is, higher energy prices could lead to higher costs for businesses and consumers, and weigh on the global and Singapore economies," said Singapore's deputy prime minister, Gan Kim Yong. 





  Write to Ying Xian Wong at yingxian.wong@wsj.com and Megan Cheah at megan.cheah@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  March 05, 2026 02:39 ET (07:39 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2026 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: /C O R R E C T I O N — Huawei/
data.description:
/C O R R E C T I O N -- Huawei/

/C O R R E C T I O N -- Huawei/

PR Newswire

BARCELONA, Spain, March 3, 2026

In the news release, Huawei Hosts the 4th Digital Economy Development Forum, issued 03-Mar-2026 by Huawei over PR Newswire, we are advised by the company that the last paragraph, 3rd sentence, should read "For more information, please visit: https://carrier.huawei.com/en/minisite/events/mwc2026/" rather than "For more information, please visit: https://carrier.huawei.com/en/events/mwc2026" as originally issued inadvertently. The complete, corrected release follows:

Huawei Hosts the 4th Digital Economy Development Forum

From Vision to Value for an Intelligent Future

BARCELONA, Spain, March 3, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- At MWC Barcelona 2026, Huawei hosted the 4th Digital Economy Development Forum, bringing together nearly 100 digital strategy makers, industry experts, and industry association representatives from across numerous regions, including Asia-Pacific, the Middle East, and Africa. Together, they discussed new opportunities for digital economy development in the age of AI.

David Wang, Huawei's Executive Director of the Board, opened the event by sharing the latest thoughts and practices on key subjects like policy, infrastructure, and talent, all centered on the event theme – From Vision to Value for an Intelligent Future.

AI is currently seeing explosive growth around the world, and AI agents have started taking center stage. Furthermore, the implementation of physical AI is accelerating. According to IDC, by 2030, AI will contribute 22.3 trillion dollars to the global economy. This is a strong indicator that the digital economy is no longer about just "connectivity + data". Rather, it is now about "AI + decision making". AI is reshaping the global economic landscape and restructuring value chains.

Based on Huawei's extensive experience in digital economy, Wang highlighted three key areas the world must address to achieve the high-quality development of the global digital economy. First, the world needs favorable industry policies that will fully support the digital economy and drive the deep and tandem development of the digital and real economies. Second, the world requires future-oriented infrastructure if the digital economy is going to thrive. Such infrastructure will lay a solid foundation for the large-scale application of digital technology and digital transformation across industries. Third, the world needs a multi-layered, sustainable talent ecosystem, which is an absolute strategic pillar for the long-term sustainable development of the digital economy.

At the conclusion of his speech, Wang stated, "The age if AI is here. And Huawei will continue working with you all to enable intelligent industries and build a solid foundation for the intelligent world. Together, let's turn vision into value and shape our intelligent future."

During the event's keynote speech session, attendees gathered to explore the future construction of digital infrastructure.

Joyce Liu, Director of Huawei's ICT Marketing Operations Department, pointed out that digital infrastructure is the cornerstone of industry development in the age of AI, meaning infrastructure construction should be advanced on all fronts, including networks, energy, and computing power. Such efforts require industry players to establish millisecond-level low-latency network connections as soon as possible, thereby preventing information silos from hindering AI development. It is also necessary to more quickly build out green data centers, thus driving compute growth while reducing carbon emissions.

Professor Rong Ke, Director of the Institute of Economics at the School of Social Sciences, Tsinghua University, gave a speech in which he emphasized that data is an essential production factor in the age of AI. Only by refining digital infrastructure throughout the entire process of data generation, transfer, storage, and application, can the world fully unleash the value of data.

The forum reached a fundamental consensus on key topics surrounding digital economy development: Countries are at different stages of digital economy development, and need to formulate AI development strategies and implementation paths that suit their own circumstances in order to efficiently promote intelligent transformation.

Since its inception in 2023, Huawei Digital Economy Development Forum has gradually grown into an innovation-driven, collaboration-based, high-level communication platform for shared global success.

MWC Barcelona 2026 will be held from March 2 to March 5 in Barcelona, Spain. During the event, Huawei will showcase its latest products and solutions at stand 1H50 in Fira Gran Via Hall 1. For more information, please visit: https://carrier.huawei.com/en/minisite/events/mwc2026/

 

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/huawei-hosts-the-4th-digital-economy-development-forum-302703347.html

SOURCE Huawei

data.title: 139th Canton Fair Set to Open as Global Partners Organize Delegations to Attend
data.description:
139th Canton Fair Set to Open as Global Partners Organize Delegations to Attend

139th Canton Fair Set to Open as Global Partners Organize Delegations to Attend

PR Newswire

GUANGZHOU, China, March 5, 2026

GUANGZHOU, China, March 5, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- The 139th Canton Fair will open on April 15, 2026. Global partners are showing strong enthusiasm as they are organizing member companies to attend. As of now, around 30 Canton Fair Global Cooperative Partners have confirmed plans to organize buyer delegations, including the United States of America-China Chamber of Commerce, China-Britain Business Council, Malaysia–China Chamber of Commerce, Peru China Chamber of Commerce, and Victorian Chamber of Commerce and Industry in Australia. Partners expect exhibitors to arrive at the Canton Fair with company profiles, product catalogs and samples in hand, ready to engage directly with buyer delegations.

To date, the Canton Fair has established cooperative relationships with 227 business associations, multinationals, and professional trade service organizations across 110 countries and regions. These partners regularly assist in organizing promotional events and outreach activities in their markets, helping member companies connect with the Canton Fair, a high-quality international sourcing platform. Member companies of the Victorian Chamber of Commerce and Industry in Australia said they plan to source smartphones, computers as well as household electric appliances at the 139th Canton Fair, in order to meet the demand in Australia's rapidly growing consumer-tech market.

For buyers, organized delegations streamline supplier discovery and compress evaluation cycles, allowing them to compare pricing, production capacity and compliance standards within a concentrated timeframe, reflecting their stronger sourcing intent and higher likelihood of deal execution. Such efficiency can reduce sourcing risk, strengthen negotiating leverage and help buyers secure reliable suppliers or adjust more quickly to shifts in market demand.

Pre-registration for overseas buyers for the 139th Canton Fair is now in full swing. Global partners and buyers from around the world will soon gather in Guangzhou to take part in this premier international trade event.

For pre-registration, please click:
https://buyer.cantonfair.org.cn/register/buyer/email?source_type=16

 

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/139th-canton-fair-set-to-open-as-global-partners-organize-delegations-to-attend-302705058.html

SOURCE Canton Fair

data.title: South Korean Stocks Have Best Day in Nearly Two Decades After Record Plunge — Update
data.description:
By Kwanwoo Jun 




  South Korean equities had their best day in nearly two decades, recovering from their record slide the previous session, as bargain hunters swooped in following panic over the Iran conflict and rising energy prices. 



  The benchmark Kospi surged 9.6% to close at 5583.90 on Thursday, its largest one-day gain since October 2008. That marked a swift, sharp rebound from Wednesday's 12% plunge--its steepest-ever drop. 



  Helping buoy sentiment were Wall Street's gains overnight as investors set aside some fears over the Middle East conflict. Analysts said traders also appeared to view the latest selloff as overdone. 



  Han Ji-young of Kiwoom Securities said the plunge was excessive even based solely on the pace of decline in local stocks. "At this point, reducing your stock holdings doesn't offer much benefit," the analyst said in a note. 



  Concerns about higher oil prices and the broader impact of the Middle East conflict on energy-reliant South Korea prompted investors to book profits after the recent strong rally in Korean stocks, fueling the sharp selloff earlier this week. 



  Still, valuations of local chip stocks, which led the Kospi rally amid the global artificial-intelligence boom, remain intact, Han said. 



  Some analysts now see a buying opportunity following the rout. 



  UBS Global Wealth Management's Chief Investment Office upgraded South Korean equities to attractive, saying the roughly 20% Kospi correction reflects "technical unwinds rather than a deterioration in fundamentals" and offers scope for dip buying. 



  "Current valuations are now more compelling," UBS said, noting that higher DRAM chip prices amid supply shortages should support robust earnings growth for Korean semiconductor makers. 



  "Importantly, this momentum is not just a short-term phenomenon, but is being driven by structural trends such as the global buildout of AI infrastructure, digital transformation and South Korea's leadership in advanced manufacturing," it said. 



  UBS said it expects DRAM spot prices to nearly double to $1.7 per gigabit by the second half of 2027. 



  The Kospi surged 76% in 2025 and remains more than 30% higher this year despite its recent volatile trade. 



  In Thursday trading, chip and auto stocks were among the biggest gainers, with index heavyweight Samsung Electronics surging 11% after slumping by a similar margin in the previous session. Its local memory-chip rival, SK Hynix, also soared 11%, and Hyundai Motor advanced 9.4%. 



  The local stock-market operator, Korea Exchange, briefly suspended trading to cool volatility following sharp gains in both the main Kospi and the smaller tech-heavy Kosdaq markets. 



  The Korean won strengthened against the dollar amid renewed risk-on sentiment, driven spurred by overnight gains on Wall Street. The greenback was at 1,468.10 won, down from 1,476.20 won in Seoul onshore trading late Wednesday. 





  Write to Kwanwoo Jun at kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  March 05, 2026 02:32 ET (07:32 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2026 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Malaysia's Central Bank Keeps Rate Unchanged
data.description:
By Ying Xian Wong 




  KUALA LUMPUR, Malaysia--Malaysia's central bank kept its benchmark interest rate unchanged Thursday for a fourth consecutive meeting, citing stable economic conditions amid external uncertainty. 



  Bank Negara Malaysia held its overnight policy rate at 2.75%, matching expectations of all eight economists polled by The Wall Street Journal. 



  The central bank last adjusted policy settings in July 2025, when it cut the rate by 25 basis points. 



  It pointed to uncertainty stemming from the conflict in the Middle East, noting the impact on global growth will depend on how the situation develops. 



  "The Malaysian economy is facing these challenges from a position of strength, with robust domestic growth, moderate inflation, sound financial sector and resilient external position," it said.
